concerns.analysis: The image manifest indicates that all extracted images have corrupt or missing base64 data. Specifically, images that have `` tags in the HTML (Figures 1-13, 19, 20) are marked as 'valid: false' with extraction errors ('No module named \'PIL\''), meaning they will not render visually. Furthermore, several meaningful charts from the source PDF (Figures 14-18, 21-23 on pages 56-58, 61-62) are completely missing `` elements in the HTML and are instead represented merely as bracketed text
concerns.output_quote:
The tags for Figures 1-13, 19, and 20 point to corrupted/invalid base64 data per the manifest. Additionally, Figures 14-18 and 21-23 lack tags entirely and only appear as bracketed text (e.g., `

[Image: Line chart showing projections...]

`).

concerns.description: Fidelity Content evaluation failed — dimension not assessed, requires human review
concerns.analysis: The content fidelity call did not produce valid results.

concerns.description: Fidelity Structural evaluation failed — dimension not assessed, requires human review
concerns.analysis: The structural fidelity call did not produce valid results.

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 4 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=4. Examples: pages 37-38: 2 cols (, season start date...); pages 41-42: 2 cols (, management area...); pages 47-48: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf, southern flounder advisory committee...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.8x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.77. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 6 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=6, examples=['https://www.implan.com', 'http://www.implan.com/', 'mailto:Michael.loeffler@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Year"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.
header_footer_count=61. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.
